duties: Landlord of dwelling let on assured / short assured tenancy MUST keep in repair: structure & exterior, installations for water/gas/electricity/sanitation, space heating & heating water
rights: Tenant entitled to works done within reasonable time
Compensation up to £4,250 available in First‑tier Tribunal Property Chamber
No requirement to prove landlord was told IF they ought reasonably to have known
remedies: Tribunal may order works without landlord consent
time_limits: 6 years from date of breach: Limitation Act 1980 s.9

plain_language: If you rent a home in England/Wales your landlord is LEGALLY REQUIRED to keep the structure, roof, walls, windows, pipes, drains, electrics, boiler, heating and hot water in good working order. Applies automatically to almost all tenancies. This is a strict duty — you do not need to prove they were careless.
official_text_excerpt: (1) The landlord is under an obligation to keep in repair—(a) the structure and exterior; (b) installations for water, gas, electricity, sanitation; (c) installations for space heating and heating water.

case_law.title: O’Brien v Robinson [1973] AC 912
case_law.note: Duty includes safety from foreseeable harm
